action: Implement a deterministic queue-state materializer that reconstructs effective state from the canonical baseline plus append-only updates without overwriting conflicts.
selection_basis: M-011 identified deterministic queue reconstruction as the highest-value unresolved continuity capability, and no newer continuity-owned queue item superseded it.
memory_recovered: Effective queue state is a provenance-preserving reconstruction, not a blind replacement: validate item ownership, order updates deterministically, retain every source digest, and preserve conflicts until explicit review.
contradictions_resolved: A deterministic materializer can coordinate shared state without modifying the canonical baseline.
A later update may extend a baseline item, but an owner conflict must be retained rather than silently resolved.
A candidate compaction is not itself canonical state or proof that referenced evidence exists.

observed: The materializer and five-test contract are committed.
Five deterministic tests passed in an isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source.
No specialist registry change was required.
inferred: The tool can prevent baseline-only false negatives and cross-team owner clobbering when supplied a complete update set.
proposed: Run the tool in an authenticated checkout over every operations/queue-updates record.
Add commit and evidence-reference verification before producing a reviewed canonical compaction.
superseded: Manual queue reconstruction without deterministic ordering or conflict retention.
unresolved: Complete queue-update enumeration through an authenticated checkout.

verification: 5 Node tests passed: materialization, deterministic order, owner mismatch rejection, owner-conflict retention, and append-only policy enforcement.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract queue semantics only; no raw private chat, health or pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.
canonical_queue_update_policy: Append-only team-owned update; operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json was not overwritten.
next_action: Enumerate all queue updates in an authenticated checkout, validate referenced outputs, and emit a candidate compaction plus conflict report for explicit review.
